title: Method and key management facility for managing keys of a single user having a plurality of mobile devices
abstract: A method and Key Management Facility (KMF) for managing keys of a single user having a plurality of devices is provided. The KMF receives an Over-The-Air Rekeying (OTAR) message relating to a first device and including an interworking bit. If the interworking bit is set, the KMF retrieves a main source RSI and a Sub-RSI field from the OTAR message. If the main source RSI matches other main source RSIs from other devices, the KMF manages keys for all devices that have the same main source RSI in an identical manner.
claims:
Claims 1. A method for managing keys of a single user having a plurality of devices, the method comprising: receiving an Over-The-Air Rekeying (OTAR) message at a Key Management Facility (KMF), the OTAR message relating to a first device and including an interworking bit; if the interworking bit is set toâONâ, retrieving by the KMF a main source RSI and a Sub-RSI field from the OTAR message; and if the main source RSI matches other main source RSIs from other devices, managing, by the KMF, keys for all devices that have the same main source RSI in an identical manner. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the OTAR message includes a Source RSI Field, and wherein the Source RSI Field comprises the main source RSI and the Sub-RSI Field. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the Sub-RSI field is linked with a Long-Term Evolution (LTE) client ID. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the main source RSI field represents a Long-Term Evolution (LTE) interworking user. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the step of managing keys for all devices that have the same main source RSI in an identical manner comprises provisioning identical keys for all devices that have the same main source RSI. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the step of managing keys for all devices that have the same main source RSI in an identical manner comprises maintaining identical key management states for all devices that have the same main source RSI. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein step of managing keys for all devices that have the same main source RSI in an identical manner comprises transmitting an identical OTAR message to all devices that have the same main source RSI. 8. A method for managing keys of a single user having a plurality of devices, the method comprising: receiving an Over-The-Air Rekeying (OTAR) message at a Key Management Facility (KMF), the OTAR message relating to a first device and including an interworking bit; if the interworking bit is set, retrieving by the KMF a main source RSI and a Sub-RSI field from the OTAR message; and if the main source RSI matches other main source RSIs from other devices, synchronizing, by the KMF, keys for all devices that have the same main source RSI in an identical manner. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the OTAR message includes a Source RSI Field, and wherein the Source RSI Field comprises the main source RSI and the Sub-RSI Field. 10. The method of claim 8, wherein the Sub-RSI field is linked with a Long-Term Evolution (LTE) client ID. 11. The method of claim 8, wherein the main source RSI field represents a Long- Term Evolution (LTE) interworking user. 12. The method of claim 8, wherein the step of synchronizing keys for all devices that have the same main source RSI in an identical manner comprises provisioning identical keys for all devices that have the same main source RSI. 13. The method of claim 8, wherein step of synchronizing keys for all devices that have the same main source RSI in an identical manner comprises transmitting an identical OTAR message to all devices that have the same main source RSI. 14. A Key Management Function (KMF) for managing keys of a single user having a plurality of devices, the KMF comprising: a processor that performs: 

 receiving an Over-The-Air Rekeying (OTAR) message at a Key Management Facility (KMF), the OTAR message relating to a first device and including an interworking bit; if the interworking bit is set, retrieving by the KMF a main source RSI and a Sub-RSI field from the OTAR message; and if the main source RSI matches other main source RSIs from other devices, managing, by the KMF, keys for all devices that have the same main source RSI in an identical manner. 15. The KMF of claim 14, wherein the OTAR message includes a Source RSI Field, and wherein the Source RSI Field comprises the main source RSI and the Sub-RSI Field. 16. The method of claim 14, wherein the Sub-RSI field is linked with a Long-Term Evolution (LTE) client ID. 17. The method of claim 14, wherein the main source RSI field represents a Long- Term Evolution (LTE) interworking user. 18. The method of claim 14, wherein the step of managing keys for all devices that have the same main source RSI in an identical manner comprises provisioning identical keys for all devices that have the same main source RSI. 19. The method of claim 14, wherein the step of managing keys for all devices that have the same main source RSI in an identical manner comprises maintaining identical key management states for all devices that have the same main source RSI. 20. The method of claim 14, wherein step of managing keys for all devices that have the same main source RSI in an identical manner comprises transmitting an identical OTAR message to all devices that have the same main source RSI.
